items.front: What exception exists to the requirement for specific radio or light-signal movement instructions?
items.back: Operations permitted in a Letter of Agreement (LOA).

items.front: What must be confirmed when a taxi clearance to a runway is issued?
items.back: Confirm the aircraft has the correct runway assignment.

items.front: What must the taxi route include?
items.back: The specific route to follow in concise and easy-to-understand terms.

items.front: What taxi clearance restriction applies to super or heavy aircraft?
items.back: Avoid clearances that require super or heavy aircraft to use greater than normal taxiing power.

items.front: What two methods are used to hold aircraft/vehicles clear of a runway?
items.back: 1) Instruct to hold short of a specific runway. 2) Instruct to hold at a specified point.

items.front: When must ILS critical area access be controlled?
items.back: When the official weather observation is ceiling less than 800 feet OR visibility less than 2 miles.

items.front: When must the POFZ be clear of traffic?
items.back: When an aircraft on a vertically-guided final approach is within 2 miles of the runway threshold AND ceiling is below 300 feet OR visibility is less than 3/4 SM.
